title: Encounter Twist
description: A complication that turns a fight into a scene — original table authored for the encounter builder.
entries: A third party is already fighting them when the party arrives — and may not be friendly either.
They aren't hostile yet. Something the party does in the first moments decides it.
One of them is {pick:wounded|shackled|clearly unwilling} and will defect if given the chance.
There is a noncombatant in the middle of it: {pick:a child|a merchant and cart|a wounded soldier|an oblivious pilgrim}.
The real danger is the ground itself: {pick:the floor is failing|a fire is spreading|the water is rising|a rockslide is one loud noise away}.
They have hostages — or something the party needs intact.
Reinforcements arrive for the enemy in {num:2-4} rounds unless the alarm is silenced.
Something much bigger is watching the fight, waiting to take the winner.
They are a decoy. The real strike is happening {pick:back at camp|at the wagon|behind the party|somewhere the party just left}.
One of them recognizes a party member — by name.
They're guarding a {pick:door|bridge|shrine|cache} they cannot abandon; they'll break off any pursuit to return to it.
The fight starts on a timer: {pick:a rope is burning|a ritual is nearly complete|a gate is closing|the tide is coming in}.
Killing their leader triggers something worse: {pick:a death curse|the thing it was keeping caged|a blood oath among the survivors}.
They are sick, and it spreads: anyone wounded risks carrying it away from the fight.
Weather turns mid-fight — {pick:fog rolls in and swallows the field|a downpour kills all torches|lightning starts striking the tallest thing standing}.
Loot lies scattered and visible mid-battlefield, tempting someone to break formation for it.
They fight over the party, not against it — two factions of them each want the party for their own purposes.
One of the party's belongings is what they came for, and they will disengage the moment they have it.
The fight wakes something below: on any very loud round, the floor thumps back.
Surrender is offered — theirs or demanded of the party — exactly when the fight turns.
An audience gathers: {pick:vultures and worse circle|locals watch from a ridge|spirits flicker at the field's edge}, and word of what happens here will travel.
They are bound to this place and cannot leave it; three steps past its border they stop dead at an invisible line.
Every one of them that falls {pick:gets back up once|dissolves into the same black smoke|whispers the same word as it dies}.
There is an obvious way to end the fight without winning it — if anyone stops swinging long enough to see it.
